# UC-18.3.12 · SDN Controller High Availability

## Description

Correlates clustered SDN manager health across Cisco ACI APIC (three-node Raft), VMware NSX Manager (three-node cluster with VIP), Cisco NDFC, and neutral REST/syslog lanes—tracking cluster_status, leader_id, election_epoch, db_sync_state, node_role, and textual split-brain or partition hints when consensus, leadership, or datastore replication diverges from steady state.

## Value

Policy and overlay forwarding depend on controllers agreeing who leads and whether replicated stores caught up; catching degraded cluster posture, rogue leaders versus CMDB intent, or replication lag early avoids silent policy publish stalls, split-brain scares, and multi-team blame loops across netops, virtualization, and automation groups.

## Implementation

Land all five sourcetypes on governed indexes; normalize cluster_key; deploy expected_sdn_ha_clusters lookup; schedule uc_18_3_12_sdn_ha_risk_core and alert clone uc_18_3_12_sdn_ha_risk_alert with maint_suppress discipline and ITSM correlation.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• **APIC three-node clusters** run **Raft** consensus; telemetry must surface cluster_status, node_role, leader_id, election_epoch, and db_sync_state so **split-brain** hints are not buried in generic fault feeds. Install **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ACI** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) where cisco:aci:cluster_health JSON arrives from **REST** polls against each controller IP (topSystem and related MO paths your operations baseline documents) on **120–300s** cadences with read-only credentials isolated from human admins.
• **NSX Manager** clusters rely on a **VIP** across three appliances; normalize cluster_status, leader_id, election_epoch when exposed, db_sync_state, and node_role inside vmware:nsx:cluster_status via **Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) against Policy and Manager HTTPS APIs with secrets in Splunk credential stanzas only.
• **Cisco NDFC** cluster services should decode to cisco:ndfc:cluster with leader_id, replica health, and datastore replication mapped into db_sync_state; drive polls through **Splunk REST API Modular Input** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) and sanity-check layout against **Cisco Data Center Networking for Splunk Platform** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) samples so dashboard tokens stay consistent.
• Neutral automation lanes append sdn:controller_cluster rows with the same canonical fields when a platform lacks a TA-specific sourcetype so operators keep **one correlated workspace** instead of per-vendor tabs.
• **network:sdn_syslog** should preserve leader, quorum, VIP, election, and partition mnemonics while scrubbing operator PII; terminate on universal forwarders or **HEC** receivers with host equal to the management FQDN.
• Co-locate **Splunk Add-on for VMware** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) when NSX or NDFC VMs live on vSphere so host maintenance windows explain benign cluster churn.
• Author **expected_sdn_ha_clusters.csv** with cluster_key, expected_leader, maint_suppress, fabric_team, and notes versioned beside **CMDB** exports.
Step 1 Configure data collection
• **cisco:aci:cluster_health** — Hit each reachable APIC member, not only the VIP, when routing allows; persist explicit healthy, degraded, or split-brain cluster_status plus leader_id, node_role (leader, follower, standby), election_epoch, and db_sync_state. Fix cluster_key strings like apic:FABRIC-A for stable joins.
• **vmware:nsx:cluster_status** — Align JSON fields such as overall_status, mgmt_cluster_status, and per-node controller_cluster_status; delay steady-state labeling **60–180s** after VIP failover scripts finish to skip transient duplicates.
• **cisco:ndfc:cluster** — Fold NDFC service health into cluster_status and db_sync_state; trim INFO floods that mask WARN at the source parser when possible.
• **sdn:controller_cluster** — Require cluster_key and platform tags even when some HA fields are null so SPL coalesce paths stay deterministic across upgrades.
• **network:sdn_syslog** — Tune TIME_PREFIX for RFC5424 timestamps; verify failover narratives land within a minute of REST observations during synthetic tests.
Additional product anchors
• **APIC Raft** tolerates a single missing controller; **cluster_status=degraded** still deserves paging when **db_sync_state** lags because policy publication may stall even if **bridging** continues.
• **NSX election epochs** can advance during benign manager restarts; pair **epoch_latest** moves with **leader_latest** diffs before declaring a **split brain** incident.
• **NDFC** exposes service-level readiness separate from **VIP** ping success; ingest both so dashboards do not show green while **replication** is paused.
• **sdn:controller_cluster** rows from automation should copy **vendor_case** identifiers into a notes field for **support** handoffs without retyping MOIDs.
• **network:sdn_syslog** may duplicate across **site collectors**; dedup keys on host, severity, and message fingerprint to avoid double counting **risk_peak**.
• **expected_leader** must track **intentional** leadership moves after **disaster recovery**; stale rows cause chronic **leader_gap** noise.
• **Certificate rotations** on **HTTPS** collectors can pause **REST** polls without touching the fabric—monitor **modinput** health indexes in parallel.
• **Timezone skew** between controllers and Splunk stretches apparent **lag**; enforce **NTP** discipline before tuning bin widths.
• **Password expiring** service accounts spike **401** responses that resemble outages—tie **credential** calendars to **rest_ta** secret rotations.
• **Brownfield onboarding** should backfill **24h** of each sourcetype before arming alerts so baselines exist for **epoch** deltas.
Step 2 Create the search
Paste the SPL from the spl field, wrap permitted index unions inside deployment macros, save **uc_18_3_12_sdn_ha_risk_core**, and clone **uc_18_3_12_sdn_ha_risk_alert** with tighter risk_peak thresholds for severity-1 paging.

```spl
(index=dc_sdn OR index=cisco_aci OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=ndfc_ops OR index=network_ops)
(
  sourcetype="sdn:controller_cluster" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:cluster_health" OR
  sourcetype="vmware:nsx:cluster_status" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:cluster" OR
  sourcetype="network:sdn_syslog"
)
earliest=-24h@h latest=now
| eval platform=case(
    sourcetype=="cisco:aci:cluster_health","apic",
    sourcetype=="vmware:nsx:cluster_status","nsx_manager",
    sourcetype=="cisco:ndfc:cluster","ndfc",
    sourcetype=="sdn:controller_cluster","aggregated_rest",
    sourcetype=="network:sdn_syslog","syslog_enriched",
    null())
| eval cluster_key=lower(trim(coalesce(
    cluster_id,cluster_name,fabric_domain,manager_cluster,
    apic_guid,ndfc_site,mgmt_cluster_id,apic_name,"")))
| eval leader_now=trim(coalesce(
    leader_id,cluster_leader,raft_leader,elected_leader,
    active_manager,vip_owner,ndfc_leader,""))
| eval role_norm=lower(trim(coalesce(
    node_role,member_role,controller_role,apic_role,
    replica_role,mgmt_node_role,"")))
| eval cluster_st=lower(trim(coalesce(
    cluster_status,ha_status,health_state,cluster_health,
    overall_status,system_status,"")))
| eval db_sync=lower(trim(coalesce(
    db_sync_state,datastore_sync,repl_state,sync_status,
    db_state,db_replication,"")))
| eval epoch=tonumber(coalesce(
    election_epoch,raft_term,consensus_generation,
    vote_epoch,generation_id,""))
| eval raw_blob=lower(concat(_raw," ",coalesce(fault_text,alarm_message,summary,"")))
| eval split_brain=if(match(raw_blob,"(?i)(split.?brain|brain.?split|partition.*cluster|minority.*partition|lost.?quorum|unable.?to.?elect)"),1,0)
| eval syslog_ha_signal=if(sourcetype=="network:sdn_syslog" AND match(lower(_raw),
    "(?i)(leader|follower|candidate|raft|quorum|vip|election|replication.?lag|datastore)"),1,0)
| eval degraded_ctrl=case(
    match(cluster_st,"degraded|critical|failed|down|error|not.?ready|offline"),1,
    match(db_sync,"out.?of.?sync|stale|lag|broken|error|unknown"),1,
    split_brain==1,1,
    role_norm IN ("unknown","idle") AND match(cluster_st,"degraded|unknown"),1,
    true(),0)
| lookup expected_sdn_ha_clusters cluster_key OUTPUTNEW expected_leader maint_suppress
| eval leader_gap=if(isnotnull(expected_leader) AND leader_now!="" AND leader_now!=expected_leader AND (isnull(maint_suppress) OR maint_suppress!="true"),1,0)
| eval ha_risk_score=case(
    split_brain==1 OR match(cluster_st,"split"),90,
    degraded_ctrl==1,70,
    leader_gap==1,55,
    syslog_ha_signal==1 AND degraded_ctrl==1,45,
    true(),0)
| where ha_risk_score>=45 OR degraded_ctrl==1 OR split_brain==1 OR leader_gap==1
| bin _time span=15m
| stats max(ha_risk_score) AS risk_peak latest(cluster_st) AS cluster_status_latest latest(leader_now) AS leader_latest latest(role_norm) AS role_latest latest(db_sync) AS db_sync_latest latest(epoch) AS epoch_latest max(split_brain) AS saw_split_hint values(platform) AS platforms_seen values(sourcetype) AS sourcetype_path earliest(_time) AS first_hit latest(_time) AS last_hit BY _time cluster_key
| eval last_hit_human=strftime(last_hit,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| sort - risk_peak last_hit
| head 200
```

**Understanding this SPL** — **platform** tags separate APIC, NSX Manager, NDFC, aggregated REST, and syslog without hostregex. **coalesce()** absorbs TA field renames across Cisco and VMware releases. **split_brain** regex captures partition language when cluster_status never flips to a clean token. **lookup** applies CMDB expected_leader with maint_suppress windows. **ha_risk_score** orders response: 90 split semantics, 70 degraded health or datastore, 55 rogue leader, 45 syslog-corroborated degradation. **15m bins** smooth HTTPS jitter while sustained faults still appear across consecutive bins.
Step 3 Validate
• **APIC** — Match Splunk leader_latest and role_latest to VISORE or moquery during a known-good hour, then repeat during rehearsed maintenance.
• **NSX** — Cluster UI states must equal cluster_status_latest within one poll; reconcile VIP DNS with cluster_key spelling.
• **NDFC** — Service diagnostics and db_sync_latest align; epoch_latest should only jump backward during documented DR.
• **Syslog fidelity** — Failover lines precede or trail REST flips by under one minute; larger skew flags transport debt, not fabric failure.
• **Drills** — With maint_suppress true, scores should rise, then fall when steady state returns.
Step 4 Operationalize
• **Single-value tiles** color risk_peak with 55 / 70 / 90 thresholds per cluster_key for NOC wallboards.
• **Timelines** stack leader_latest changes with epoch_latest for election forensics.
• **Tables** export platforms_seen, sourcetype_path, cluster_status_latest, db_sync_latest, saw_split_hint, and timestamps for ITSM.
• **Drilldowns** launch Cisco DC Networking **7777** reference dashboards using tokens, never embedded credentials.
• **Runbooks** name accountable fabric, virtualization, and Splunk platform owners plus escalation paths when datastore lag outlasts maintenance.
Step 5 Operationalize & Troubleshoot
**Visualization** — Build a **status grid** of cluster_key versus risk_peak, a **timeline** for leadership and epoch markers, **ITSM annotations** for change correlation, and an optional **VIP reachability** sparkline faded when cluster_status_latest remains healthy for two consecutive bins.
**Alert design** — Schedule **10 minutes** over earliest=-45m@m; fire when risk_peak≥70 persists across **two consecutive** runs or saw_split_hint=1; **throttle 45 minutes** per cluster_key; honor maint_suppress and ITSM change tags; include cluster_key, leader_latest, expected_leader, cluster_status_latest, db_sync_latest, sourcetype_path, platforms_seen without **API secrets** in the payload.
**Troubleshooting** — Persistent leader_gap with a healthy vendor UI implies wrong cluster_key or stale lookup rows—diff CMDB. degraded_ctrl without syslog hints TA parsing drift—confirm **4022** and **4856** compatibility matrices. Empty bins during outages usually mean **rest_ta** queue backup or indexer lag—prove ingest before opening a fabric bridge. **db_sync lag** with outwardly healthy status often tracks backup or upgrade tasks—confirm with ancillary audit searches outside this core SPL.

## SPL

```spl
(index=dc_sdn OR index=cisco_aci OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=ndfc_ops OR index=network_ops)
(
  sourcetype="sdn:controller_cluster" OR sourcetype="cisco:aci:cluster_health" OR
  sourcetype="vmware:nsx:cluster_status" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndfc:cluster" OR
  sourcetype="network:sdn_syslog"
)
earliest=-24h@h latest=now
| eval platform=case(
    sourcetype=="cisco:aci:cluster_health","apic",
    sourcetype=="vmware:nsx:cluster_status","nsx_manager",
    sourcetype=="cisco:ndfc:cluster","ndfc",
    sourcetype=="sdn:controller_cluster","aggregated_rest",
    sourcetype=="network:sdn_syslog","syslog_enriched",
    null())
| eval cluster_key=lower(trim(coalesce(
    cluster_id,cluster_name,fabric_domain,manager_cluster,
    apic_guid,ndfc_site,mgmt_cluster_id,apic_name,"")))
| eval leader_now=trim(coalesce(
    leader_id,cluster_leader,raft_leader,elected_leader,
    active_manager,vip_owner,ndfc_leader,""))
| eval role_norm=lower(trim(coalesce(
    node_role,member_role,controller_role,apic_role,
    replica_role,mgmt_node_role,"")))
| eval cluster_st=lower(trim(coalesce(
    cluster_status,ha_status,health_state,cluster_health,
    overall_status,system_status,"")))
| eval db_sync=lower(trim(coalesce(
    db_sync_state,datastore_sync,repl_state,sync_status,
    db_state,db_replication,"")))
| eval epoch=tonumber(coalesce(
    election_epoch,raft_term,consensus_generation,
    vote_epoch,generation_id,""))
| eval raw_blob=lower(concat(_raw," ",coalesce(fault_text,alarm_message,summary,"")))
| eval split_brain=if(match(raw_blob,"(?i)(split.?brain|brain.?split|partition.*cluster|minority.*partition|lost.?quorum|unable.?to.?elect)"),1,0)
| eval syslog_ha_signal=if(sourcetype=="network:sdn_syslog" AND match(lower(_raw),
    "(?i)(leader|follower|candidate|raft|quorum|vip|election|replication.?lag|datastore)"),1,0)
| eval degraded_ctrl=case(
    match(cluster_st,"degraded|critical|failed|down|error|not.?ready|offline"),1,
    match(db_sync,"out.?of.?sync|stale|lag|broken|error|unknown"),1,
    split_brain==1,1,
    role_norm IN ("unknown","idle") AND match(cluster_st,"degraded|unknown"),1,
    true(),0)
| lookup expected_sdn_ha_clusters cluster_key OUTPUTNEW expected_leader maint_suppress
| eval leader_gap=if(isnotnull(expected_leader) AND leader_now!="" AND leader_now!=expected_leader AND (isnull(maint_suppress) OR maint_suppress!="true"),1,0)
| eval ha_risk_score=case(
    split_brain==1 OR match(cluster_st,"split"),90,
    degraded_ctrl==1,70,
    leader_gap==1,55,
    syslog_ha_signal==1 AND degraded_ctrl==1,45,
    true(),0)
| where ha_risk_score>=45 OR degraded_ctrl==1 OR split_brain==1 OR leader_gap==1
| bin _time span=15m
| stats max(ha_risk_score) AS risk_peak latest(cluster_st) AS cluster_status_latest latest(leader_now) AS leader_latest latest(role_norm) AS role_latest latest(db_sync) AS db_sync_latest latest(epoch) AS epoch_latest max(split_brain) AS saw_split_hint values(platform) AS platforms_seen values(sourcetype) AS sourcetype_path earliest(_time) AS first_hit latest(_time) AS last_hit BY _time cluster_key
| eval last_hit_human=strftime(last_hit,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| sort - risk_peak last_hit
| head 200
```

## Visualization

(1) Single-value grid of risk_peak per cluster_key with 55/70/90 coloring. (2) Timeline of leader_latest with epoch_latest markers. (3) Table of platforms_seen, sourcetype_path, cluster_status_latest, db_sync_latest, saw_split_hint, last_hit_human. (4) ITSM annotation overlay. (5) Optional VIP reachability sparkline when healthy.

## Known False Positives

**planned_controller_swaps** — Rolling APIC replacements or NSX Manager VM refreshes deliberately walk nodes through standby transitions; Splunk may show leader_latest churn and short db_sync_state noise while Raft or Corfu-style stores catch up. Require maint_suppress on expected_sdn_ha_clusters or an ITSM change token before paging, and confirm cluster_status_latest returns to healthy across two polling windows.

**vip_dns_ttl_caching** — After VIP failovers, stale DNS on a collector can point HTTPS polls at the wrong appliance, fabricating leader_gap while the vendor UI on correct IPs looks clean. Flush resolver caches on rest_ta hosts, validate forward and reverse DNS against the VIP, and compare per-member polls before escalating fabric teams.

**certificate_rotation_blip** — When APIC or NSX client-auth certificates rotate, REST modular inputs can return SSL handshake failures that resemble cluster outage in logs even though db_sync_state is fine. Watch rest_ta health metrics and align rotation ceremonies with Splunk credential updates to avoid false bridges.

**lab_chaos_injection** — Chaos drills that force partitioned networks or delayed heartbeats generate split-brain text without production impact. Tag environment=lab or route those indexes to a non-paged workspace.

**telemetry_sampling_skips** — Aggressive sample filters on network:sdn_syslog can drop leader change lines while REST still captures state transitions, creating syslog_ha_signal gaps. Validate props.conf LINE_BREAKER and TRANSFORMS that might truncate multi-line HA traps.

**ndfc_service_recycle** — NDFC minor service bounces can spike cisco:ndfc:cluster warnings that self-heal within minutes; correlate with Cisco maintenance advisories and require sustained risk_peak across three bins before sev-1.

**apic_readonly_rbac_limits** — Some read-only APIC accounts cannot reach every MO path used for cluster health JSON, producing empty cisco:aci:cluster_health rows that look like missing controllers. Test AAA domains and capabilities separately from the Splunk service account before alert go-live.
